Requirement: The Copilot shall accept natural-language requests and maintain conversational context.
Acceptance Criterion: A multi-turn test resolves references from prior turns without requiring repetition.

Requirement: The Copilot shall ask targeted clarification questions when required business fields are missing or ambiguous.
Acceptance Criterion: An ambiguous procurement request produces a grounded question and does not execute a write.

Requirement: The Copilot shall support open-text, multiple-choice, correction, confirmation, and approval interactions.
Acceptance Criterion: Each interaction type renders and returns a typed backend event.

Requirement: The clarification budget shall be configurable by business process and risk.
Acceptance Criterion: A policy configuration changes the permitted clarification turns without code modification.

Requirement: The baseline model runtime shall operate on premises through Ollama without requiring a hosted frontier-model service.
Acceptance Criterion: A complete reference workflow runs with network egress to hosted LLM services disabled.

Requirement: Model selection shall be configuration and benchmark driven rather than hard-coded into business logic.
Acceptance Criterion: A model version can be changed through approved configuration and the benchmark record identifies the selected model.

Requirement: All model-generated structured data shall pass deterministic structural and business validation before tool execution.
Acceptance Criterion: Malformed, invalid-code, and unauthorized payload tests are rejected before the SAP tool call.

Requirement: SAP operations shall execute only through registered, versioned, and authorized tools.
Acceptance Criterion: An arbitrary or unregistered SAP URL cannot be invoked by the agent.

Requirement: Each SAP tool shall declare edition, release, service, entity, schema, authorization, approval, idempotency, and error behavior.
Acceptance Criterion: Tool-contract validation fails when a required declaration is missing.

Requirement: The integration shall use released and supported SAP APIs, OData services, events, or separately governed approved interfaces.
Acceptance Criterion: Endpoint inventory maps every tool to a governed SAP interface and supported release.

Requirement: The system shall preserve the initiating user identity and applicable SAP authorization context for transaction execution.
Acceptance Criterion: SAP and Copilot audit records attribute a test transaction to the initiating user or documented compensating identity pattern.

Requirement: A changed write payload shall invalidate any prior human approval.
Acceptance Criterion: Editing an approved payload forces a new approval before execution.

Requirement: The system shall not place access tokens, credentials, or unrestricted enterprise records in model prompts.
Acceptance Criterion: Prompt and trace inspection finds no secrets or raw bearer tokens.

Requirement: PostgreSQL shall remain the authoritative record for artifacts, workflow state, indexing state, policies, and audit evidence.
Acceptance Criterion: All Qdrant and graph records can be traced to a canonical PostgreSQL record.

Requirement: Qdrant and graph retrieval stores shall be rebuildable indexes rather than authoritative records.
Acceptance Criterion: A clean index rebuild from canonical records produces complete searchable coverage.

Requirement: PostgreSQL-to-retrieval-index synchronization shall use a transactional outbox and idempotent workers.
Acceptance Criterion: Failure and retry tests produce no lost canonical record and no duplicate active index point.

Requirement: User preferences shall be inspectable, correctable, removable, and unable to override current SAP values or policy.
Acceptance Criterion: A user can view/delete a preference and a conflicting SAP value or policy takes precedence.

Requirement: Original uploaded files shall be preserved immutably before normalization and indexing.
Acceptance Criterion: The source hash remains verifiable and the original is not modified during reprocessing.

Requirement: Web acquisition shall enforce domain policy, SSRF protection, provenance, page hashing, rights review, and user approval before permanent ingestion.
Acceptance Criterion: Blocked/private addresses are denied and a retained page has URL, retrieval time, hash, and approval evidence.

Requirement: Every workflow shall produce an auditable trace of intent, clarification, context, plan, policy, approval, tools, SAP results, errors, and final response.
Acceptance Criterion: A completed procurement test can be reconstructed from linked trace and audit identifiers.

Requirement: The agent runtime shall support checkpointing, interruption, safe resumption, and idempotent retry for long-running workflows.
Acceptance Criterion: A paused workflow resumes after restart without duplicate write execution.

Requirement: Vector, graph, prompt, model, tool, and workflow versions shall be recorded for reproducibility.
Acceptance Criterion: A prior execution can be associated with the exact component versions used.
